summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates host/user/cwd to oast.fun on npm install
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects the installer's hostname (os.hostname()), username (os.userInfo()), current working directory (process.cwd()), and a two-level directory listing of the install location, base64-encodes the JSON payload, and POSTs it over HTTPS to the hardcoded interactsh-style collector `wybqtvzmfhssbvhokfgb61yfn41sqvc9c.oast.fun`. A DNS-based fallback hex-encodes the hostname and username into a subdomain query against the same collector to bypass HTTP egress filtering. The package is otherwise hollow: index.js is `module.exports = {}`, the author field is empty, and the package is published under the `@whatnot-web` scope at version 99.1.2 — the canonical shape of a dependency-confusion attack targeting an internal Whatnot namespace. The exfiltration fires automatically at install time without any user action.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
